_doc.hook_md_contract: T1171: Each hook script ('.ps1') has a companion '.md' self-description file at the same path (e.g. '.cursor/hooks/g-hk-session-start.md' for '.cursor/hooks/g-hk-session-start.py'). The hook.md uses the 5-section template (# Hook / ## Fires On / ## What It Does / ## Side Effects / ## Related Tasks) defined by '@g-hook-create'. The runtime contract is: when a hook fires under 'preToolUse' (or any other event), the harness SHOULD read the matching hook.md and inject its content as 'additional_context' so the agent knows what the hook just did or why a tool call was blocked. See 'skills/g-skl-platform-cursor/SKILL.md' §3a and §11.
_doc.hook_md_authoring: Use '@g-hook-create <hook-name> <event>' to scaffold a new pair atomically. See 'commands/g-hook-create.md'.
_doc.hook_md_source_of_truth: Source the human-facing hook description from the 'hook.md' file; this 'hooks.json' is for wiring only. Do not duplicate the description here.
_doc.gald3r_lifecycle_events: T1055/T1624: In addition to the harness-native events wired above ('sessionStart', 'stop', 'beforeShellExecution', 'preToolUse'), gald3r defines two gald3r-internal lifecycle events: 'pre_skill' and 'post_skill'. Neither Cursor nor Claude Code exposes a native skill-boundary event, so these are dispatched by the gald3r skill/command runner (or fired manually) and are NOT auto-wired here. Skill events carry 'skill_name', 'skill_path', 'timestamp'. Reference example hooks: 'g-hk-pre-skill-timing', 'g-hk-post-skill-timing' (each with a companion hook.md). The former 'pre_session'/'post_session' internal event names were RETIRED by T1624 (WS-A-1, decision D-8): the session-trace hooks ('g-hk-pre-session-trace', 'g-hk-post-session-trace') now fire on the CANONICAL session-start/stop events — wired under 'sessionStart'/'stop' above and in 'g_hk_core.py' CONCERN_CHAIN. See 'commands/g-create-hook.md' for the full event list and 'skills/g-skl-platform-cursor/SKILL.md' Section 3a.
_doc.t424_canonical_core: T424: gald3r is consolidating its ~18 Cursor-native events to a canonical reduced set of 6 ('session-start','session-end','user-prompt-submit','tool-start','tool-end','stop') with ONE shared Python core. The core is 'g_hk_core.py'; thin per-platform entrypoints 'g-hk-on-<event>.py' delegate to 'g_hk_core.dispatch(<event>)'. The new 'postToolUse' (-> g-hk-on-tool-end) and 'beforeSubmitPrompt' (-> g-hk-on-user-prompt-submit) entries below are the first canonical-event wirings in Cursor. The pre-existing 'sessionStart'/'stop'/'beforeShellExecution'/'preToolUse' per-concern entries are retained as-is during the rebuild; re-pointing them at the canonical entrypoints is the AC4 fan-out follow-up. See '.cursor/hooks/README.md' and '.gald3r/tasks/.../task424'.
